channel.title: Tune Clinic Blog
channel.description: Evidence-based aesthetic medicine insights from the physicians at Tune Clinic.

item.title: Pico Fractional Laser Explained
item.description: A physician answers common questions about pico fractional lasers — how they work, what they treat, what they don't, and when they're the right choice.

item.title: Exosomes in Aesthetic Medicine
item.description: How exosomes — nanoscale vesicles that reprogram cell behavior — are reshaping regenerative aesthetics for skin and hair, explained by a physician.

item.title: How Natural-Looking Change Is Actually Created
item.description: Why natural-looking results depend less on doing more, and more on range, sequence, restraint, and facial harmony — explained by a physician.

item.title: Skin Aging Is Structural, Not Surface
item.description: Aging doesn't start with wrinkles. A physician explains why the real transformation begins beneath — in collagen, fat, and structural support.

item.title: Juvelook Volume Clinical Applications
item.description: How Juvelook Volume (PDLLA + non-crosslinked HA) addresses four anatomical aging patterns through structural biostimulation, not filler logic.

item.title: Why Lifting Comparisons Mislead Patients
item.description: Ultherapy vs. Thermage debates miss the point. The real question isn't which device is better — it's which problem you're actually trying to solve.

item.title: Ultherapy vs. Thermage vs. Oligio
item.description: Ultherapy, Thermage FLX, and Oligio are not interchangeable. A physician explains which layer each device targets and how to choose based on your anatomy.

item.title: Same Treatment, Different Results
item.description: Why two patients can receive the same aesthetic procedure and walk away with completely different experiences — and what actually determines the outcome.

item.title: Define the Standard Before the Result
item.description: Why 'Is this treatment effective?' is the wrong first question in aesthetic medicine — and what to ask instead before making a decision.

item.title: Why Filler Fears Are Outdated
item.description: A physician explains why common filler fears — migration, overfilling, long-term damage — usually reflect older techniques, not modern evidence-based practice.
